array.len.description: Returns the current length of a GC array value.

array.new_default.description: Allocates a GC array using the surrounding expected `array[T]` type and fills it with the type default for `T`. `array[T].new_default(len)` remains valid when the element type is written explicitly.

array.copy.signature: array.copy(dst, dst_index, src, src_index, len) void
array.copy.description: Copies a contiguous range from one GC array into another.

array.fill.description: Writes the same value into a contiguous GC array range.

i31.new.description: Boxes a 31-bit integer payload into a Wasm GC i31 reference.

i31.get_s.description: Reads an i31 payload back as a signed 32-bit integer.

i31.get_u.description: Reads an i31 payload back as an unsigned 32-bit integer.

ref.null.description: Constructs a null reference literal for a nullable UTU reference type.

ref.is_null.description: Returns whether a reference value is null.

ref.as_non_null.description: Reinterprets a nullable reference as non-null when the program guarantees it.

ref.eq.description: Compares two reference values for identity equality.

array.description: Builtin namespace for GC array allocation, bulk updates, and length queries.

i31.description: Builtin namespace for boxing and unboxing Wasm GC i31 references.

ref.description: Builtin namespace for nullable and reference-oriented helpers.

bool.description: Boolean scalar represented as an `i32` with `0` and `1` semantics.

eqref.description: Structurally comparable Wasm GC reference type.

externref.description: Opaque host reference, typically used for JS interop values.

i31.description: Wasm GC small tagged integer reference.

null.description: The null branch of a UTU exclusive disjunction or nullable reference.

str.description: Host-backed string reference. Use `escape` or explicit host imports for string-specific behavior.

u32.description: 32-bit integer using unsigned operations where applicable.

u64.description: 64-bit integer using unsigned operations where applicable.

void.description: Explicitly marks a function or function type as returning no value.

assert.description: Traps when the condition is false. Common inside UTU tests.

bench.description: Declares a benchmark that is synthesized into a callable export in bench mode. The generated export receives an iteration count; `setup` runs once and `measure` runs inside the timing loop.

construct.description: Instantiates a compile-time module. The aliased form creates a qualified namespace; the bare form opens that namespace into the current scope.

emit.description: Exits the current block expression early and yields the given value.

else.description: Provides the fallback branch of an `if` expression or the unwrap fallback operator.

export.description: Marks a UTU function as exported from the compiled Wasm module.

escape.description: Declares an inline host-backed helper implemented by embedded JavaScript.

shimport.description: Declares a host import available to the compiled Wasm module.

fun.description: Declares a UTU function. Lowers directly to a Wasm `(func ...)`.

get.description: Inside a protocol body, declares a getter member. A protocol getter is still a dedicated table-backed `call_indirect` entry. The contract is absolute: this syntax must produce that Wasm structure and only that structure.

set.description: Inside a protocol body, declares a setter member. A protocol setter gets its own dispatch table and is invoked with `call_indirect`, just like any other protocol member. The contract is absolute: this syntax must produce that Wasm structure and only that structure.

proto.signature: proto Name[T] { method(T, ...) return_type, get field: Type, set field: Type, ... };
proto.description: Declares a top-level protocol surface for table-backed dispatch on tagged receivers. A protocol member is the source-language name for a dedicated Wasm table plus tag-indexed `call_indirect`; methods, getters, and setters all follow that rule. The contract is absolute: this syntax must produce that structure and only that structure in Wasm. In v1, the type parameter may only appear as the first method parameter.

for.description: Iterates over a range and binds the loop capture inside the body. `..<` excludes the upper bound, while `...` includes it.

let.description: Promotes a value into a reusable binding.

match.description: Matches scalar values against literal arms. Dense scalar matches lower to a Wasm `br_table`; sparse matches fall back to comparisons.

alt.signature: alt value { name: Type => expr, _ => expr }
alt.description: Dispatches over variants and refines the matched value by type. Lowers to Wasm GC type tests with `br_on_cast`-style branching.

mod.description: Declares a compile-time namespace/template. It is expanded away before codegen rather than lowering to a separate Wasm module.

measure.description: Defines the benchmark body that executes inside the generated timing loop.

mut.description: Marks a struct field as mutable.

promote.description: Promotes a nullable value into a non-null binding for the then branch. Lowers to an explicit null check plus `ref.as_non_null` before entering the then branch.

rec.signature: rec struct Name { ... } / rec type Name = ...
rec.description: Wraps recursive GC type declarations in a Wasm `(rec ...)` group so they can reference themselves.

tag.description: Marks a struct as tagged so protocol dispatch can read its hidden runtime tag. When a tagged struct declares protocols, that clause is the explicit opt-in for table-backed protocol dispatch. Lowering prepends a hidden first `__tag` field used to index protocol tables for `call_indirect`.

while.description: Repeats while the condition stays truthy, or forever with empty parentheses.

setup.description: Defines one-time benchmark setup that runs before the measured loop.

struct.description: Declares a Wasm GC struct type. Lowers to a Wasm `(type $Name (struct ...))`.

test.description: Declares a test case that is synthesized into a zero-argument export in test mode.

type.description: Declares a sum type and its variants. Lowers to a root Wasm GC struct subtype plus one struct subtype per variant.

fatal.description: Traps immediately and is commonly used as a force-unwrap fallback.
